task_completion.description: Open the dwanko_run.tnz scene in OpenToonz
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenToonz application window is visible, and the title bar, xsheet, or scene browser shows 'dwanko_run' loaded.

task_completion.description: Set the output or camera resolution to 256x256
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Output Settings' or 'Camera Settings' dialog in OpenToonz is open, with both Width and Height explicitly set to 256 pixels.

task_completion.description: Set the output path to the frames directory and format to transparent PNG
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Output Settings' dialog shows the 'Save In' path set to '/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/spritesheet_frames/' (or similar relative path), the file format set to 'png', and settings indicating an Alpha channel or transparency is enabled.

task_completion.description: Render the animation frames to the specified directory
task_completion.visual_evidence: A rendering progress bar is visible in OpenToonz, or a file manager/terminal window shows multiple PNG files generated inside the 'spritesheet_frames' directory.

task_completion.description: Assemble the rendered frames into a 6-column spritesheet
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal window showing a command (like ImageMagick's 'montage' with a 6-column tile argument, e.g., '-tile 6x'), a script, or an image editor being used to stitch the frames into a grid, saving the final output to '/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/spritesheet.png'.

integrity.description: The agent used the OpenToonz GUI to configure and render the frames
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show the OpenToonz graphical interface being interacted with (e.g., opening menus, changing settings), not just terminal commands bypassing the software.

integrity.description: The agent actively assembled the frames into a spritesheet
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be evidence of a command, script, or tool being executed to stitch the frames together, rather than just moving or renaming a pre-existing spritesheet file.

integrity.description: The agent used the correct source animation file
integrity.visual_evidence: The animation visible in the OpenToonz viewer should match a running character (dwanko), confirming the correct sample file was used.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The correct scene path is '/home/ga/OpenToonz/samples/dwanko_run.tnz'. The agent must render 256x256 pixel transparent PNG frames to '/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/spritesheet_frames/' (the source has 72 frames, at least 10 must be rendered). The final assembled spritesheet must be saved to '/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/spritesheet.png' and must be arranged in a 6-column grid.
